current_chart_value: This is a case of 72-year-old female patient being considered for admission to home health care following her recent hospitalization for persistent dizziness and headaches, which had significantly impacted her ability to perform activities of daily living (ADLs). Her primary diagnosis is vertigo, with secondary diagnoses including essential hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and abdominal aortic ectasia.
